🎨Design System
Relowa POC · v0.1
Home / ADR-0001

ADR-0001 — Postgres as System of Record

StatusAccepted
Date2026-05-09
Decision-makersOzan (lead), Güneş (product)

Context

Relowa is a B2B platform with relational, transactional data: organizations, users, tenders, bids, escrow holdings, audit trails. We need a System of Record that:

  • Enforces relational integrity
  • Supports row-level security (RLS)
  • Has battle-tested replication and backup
  • Has a healthy ecosystem of operational tooling
  • Is portable across providers

Decision

💡
PostgreSQL is the system of record. All authoritative state lives in Postgres. In production: RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ in eu-central-1. Locally: Postgres 18-alpine in Docker. Everything else (S3 archives, EventBridge event log, PostHog analytics, materialized views) is derived data — rebuildable from Postgres if necessary.

Consequences

✓ Positive
  • One source of truth — no consistency reconciliation across stores
  • RLS handles authorization in the database layer — application bugs cannot escape
  • Postgres extensions cover most needs: TimescaleDB for time-series, PostGIS for geo, pg_cron for scheduling, pgvector for embeddings, pg_trgm for fuzzy search
  • Standard backup/PITR semantics on RDS
  • Portable — swap RDS for Aurora, Supabase Cloud, or self-host without code change
✗ Negative
  • Single-leader writes is a future scaling ceiling — acceptable through Phase 3 at projected scale
  • Operating Postgres yourself is non-trivial — mitigated by RDS Multi-AZ

Alternatives considered

Alternative Verdict Rationale
DynamoDB Rejected Multi-tenant RLS is not a thing; B2B authorization patterns become custom application logic
MongoDB Rejected No equivalent of RLS, weaker transactional guarantees
Supabase Cloud Viable Phase 1 Viable for Phase 1, but management is offsite and the data plane is shared

Reference

Designing Data-Intensive Applications — Ch. 12 "Deriving data": single source of truth, derivable everything else.

📎 See also
ADR-0002 — Realtime Standalone ADR-0003 — RLS JWT-via-GUC Multi-Tenancy
